focus: final-pass closure: tests/docs/startup contracts for @code LEAF synthesis

summary: Agent registry tests pin orchestrator-first creation, default agent count, and mode classification: orchestrator primary, council all, subagents subagent, councillor hidden/internal.
designImplication: @code should define user-callable/subagent/hidden/dual-mode status as registry metadata, not only prompt prose.

summary: Council access is guarded twice: generated permissions allow council_session only for council, and the tool checks toolContext.agent at runtime.
designImplication: @code restricted capabilities should use deny-by-default permission maps plus runtime caller guards.

summary: Council manager dispatches councillors through direct child sessions with parentID, agent=councillor prompt bodies, task tool disabled, and finally-abort cleanup; tests pin variant, title, prompt, agent field, and abort behavior.
designImplication: If @code is LEAF, nested delegation should be disabled structurally in its session/tool payloads.

summary: Depth tracking is independent session-graph state: unknown sessions are depth 0, child depth is parent+1, excessive depth returns false, and cleanup removes session depth; council returns Subagent depth exceeded without launching councillors when parent is max depth.
designImplication: LEAF guarantees should be backed by session lineage and depth enforcement.

summary: Available skills are filtered at prompt-send time based on the current agent's permission.skill rules; tests pin exact allow, empty deny, wildcard/exclusion, default orchestrator, multi-message filtering, and non-caching of final block text.
designImplication: @code should see only allowed skills in prompt context; do not rely on a global skills list plus instructions.

summary: Docs confirm the runtime model: skills are prompt-based tool configurations with wildcard/exclusion assignment; council has separate synthesizer and fan-out model layers; orchestrator delegates to council sparingly for high-stakes ambiguity; recursive council chains should be avoided.
designImplication: @code docs should describe agent layer, tool layer, session layer, and skill layer in the same terms as enforcement code.

summary: src/index.ts centralizes plugin registration and hook order: agents/tools/MCPs/config/event/tool hooks/chat hooks are registered in one returned plugin object; messages transform runs mention rewrite, image handling, todo continuation, task-session manager, phase reminder, then skill filtering.
designImplication: @code capability registration should have one startup composition point with deliberate hook order and health checks.

summary: The orchestrator prompt is dynamic and filtered, but it explicitly teaches delegation and parallel child-session branches; this is useful as a contrast, not a template, for a LEAF @code agent.
designImplication: Do not copy orchestrator delegation affordances into @code; expose bounded code-work capability without child-agent dispatch.
